condition_specific: Brain, skin, digestive system, hematological system, genitourinary system cancers
notes: Applies to full-time firefighters employed minimum 1,600 hours annually. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal substantial evidence of the condition. Employer may rebut by preponderance of medical evidence.

condition_specific: Coronary thrombosis, cerebral vascular accident, myocardial infarction, cardiac arrest (excludes hypertension and angina)
notes: Requires 5 years continuous full-time employment. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event (fire suppression, rescue, hazmat, EMS, disaster relief, or other emergency response). Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event. Benefits offset by WC, pension, Social Security, retirement, and other employer-paid income. Maximum $250,000 per event. This is a benefit statute under Title 29, not a WC presumption under Title 8.

condition_specific: Coronary thrombosis, cerebral vascular accident, myocardial infarction, cardiac arrest (excludes hypertension and angina)
notes: Employer may (not shall) provide equivalent benefits for volunteer firefighters. Requires 5 years continuous service with the same employer. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event. Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event.

filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that exposure did not occur on the job.

filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Statute covers 'emergency services providers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.

filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Statute covers 'peace officers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.
